mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and discount future cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows or positive returns exceed 55% during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and higher recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and housing demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production above 108.0 reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day equity returns in >50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 290.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with >65% probability when CPIAUCSL breaches the 290.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth below 1.5 percent combined with declining real personal consumption expenditures signals a synchronized demand contraction that depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns fail to materialize in >55% of periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS exhibit weakness.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indicates tightening supply constraints and rising input costs that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Falling industrial production correlates positively with 20-day forward returns or shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).
